title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
summary: Cursor and Baseten representatives discuss scaling coding agents to run at scale (128 agents in parallel), agent-to-agent communication and review mechanisms, and systems-level approaches to agent orchestration beyond simple parallelization.
writeup: This Cursor video features Cursor and Baseten representatives discussing how to run coding agents at scale, specifically 128 agents in parallel. The conversation covers agent-to-agent communication and review mechanisms, plus systems-level approaches to orchestration that go beyond simple parallelization. The relevance lies in treating multi-agent coding as an orchestration and infrastructure problem rather than just spawning more concurrent workers. Key threads include how agents coordinate, review each other's output, and apply test-time compute across large parallel fleets.
